verdict.answer_first: A opens with the direct answer: "lets a third-party app get access...without ever seeing their password"; B also answers first but starts with a broader label, "most common and secure".
verdict.decisiveness: A commits cleanly to the mechanism and the two main mistakes; B is solid but ends by handing off to another example: "Want me to show..."
verdict.fabrication: B makes a stronger uncited standards claim: "OAuth 2.1 makes it mandatory"; A's concrete standard citation is limited to "RFC 7636" for PKCE.
verdict.actionability: A is easier to act on because it gives exact request parameters, backend exchange details, why the split matters, and the two common implementation failures.
